---
title: "Testing Standard"
subtitle: "What the automated suite guarantees, what it covers, and how to run it"
---

::: {.page-context}
::: {.pc-crumb}
Governance <span class="sep">›</span> Testing Standard
:::
::: {.pc-supports}
**This page answers "can I trust the numbers?"** It documents the checks that guard every calculation.
:::
::: {.pc-row}
<span class="chip chip-grade-b no-dot">Green suite required</span>
<span class="pc-next">[Read the CIS standard](../cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md)</span>
:::
:::

Under [CIS §16](../cis/commercial-intelligence-specification.md), no score or recommendation is
trusted unless the test suite is green. Tests guard the *logic and structure* of the project — a red
suite is a hard stop, not a warning.

## What the suite guarantees

::: {.purpose-grid}
::: {.pg-cell}
::: {.pg-label}
Range safety
:::
::: {.pg-val}
Every scoring function returns a value in **0–100**, on full and partial rows.
:::
:::
::: {.pg-cell}
::: {.pg-label}
Missing-value safety
:::
::: {.pg-val}
Missing, null, or non-numeric inputs never crash a score — they fall back to a neutral midpoint.
:::
:::
::: {.pg-cell}
::: {.pg-label}
Structural validation
:::
::: {.pg-val}
Required columns present, identity columns unique, no negative scores where the scale forbids them.
:::
:::
::: {.pg-cell}
::: {.pg-label}
Evidence integrity
:::
::: {.pg-val}
Evidence weighting, strength classification, and per-claim aggregation match known inputs.
:::
:::
:::

## What each test file covers

| Test file | Covers |
|---|---|
| `tests/test_scoring.py` | Market/customer/AI scores stay in 0–100; missing values are safe; weights behave (higher pain ↑, penalties ↓) |
| `tests/test_validation.py` | Score-range predicate; required-columns check; negative-score guard |
| `tests/test_evidence.py` | Evidence weight = quality × bias; strength classification bands; per-claim aggregation and sorting |
| `tests/test_data_integrity.py` | Sample data loads; required columns present; ids unique; computed scores in range |

## How to run it

```bash
python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
pytest
```

The suite currently holds **53 tests**, all passing. Add a test whenever you add a scoring function or
a framework — assert its 0–100 range and its missing-value safety at minimum.

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple" icon=false}
**Scope, honestly.** Tests verify that the *code and structure* are correct. They do **not** verify
that the *synthetic inputs* reflect reality — that is what the [Evidence](../evidence/index.qmd)
section and the eventual pilots are for.
:::
